title: Dead Lettering, Poison Messages, Delivery Limits, and Parking · Inference Brief: Dead Letter Trigger Route
summary: A queue hardcodes a missing DLX, cycles messages indefinitely, drops quorum poison messages after the default delivery limit, and treats a dead-letter queue as an unowned archive. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dead letter trigger route increment in dead-letter, poison, and remediation topology. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
Record original identity, attempt, reason, first and next time, owner, budget, terminal disposition, and causal metrics without high-cardinality leaks.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Dead Letter Trigger Route · Theory Dead Letter Trigger Route
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 2927 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to route exhausted poison messages through a bound, owned, and retained remediation path; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace nack or reject without requeue, expiry, length overflow, and quorum delivery-limit dead lettering through exchange and routing policy.
